label: no
cot_prefix: Okay, so the user is asking if a man can legally leave all his money to his widow's sister, whom he plans to marry next year. Let me start by breaking this down. First, the man is currently married. He has a wife, and he's leaving all his money to her sister, whom he's planning to marry after his current wife passes. That seems a bit odd, but maybe there's a specific legal arrangement here. I need to think about inheritance laws. In many jurisdictions, the distribution of a deceased person's estate is governed by either the deceased's will or, if there's no will, by intestacy laws. So, if the man has a will that leaves everything to his future wife (his current wife's sister), then the question is whether that will is valid. But here's the catch: the man is currently married to someone else. If he writes a will that leaves everything to his future wife, is that permissible?
